overview: Orzhov Humans is a focused beatdown archetype that wins by populating the board with a large number of synergistic creatures. It relies on a stable manabase, a core of lords for collective buffs, and early aggressive threats to apply immediate pressure. The strategy emphasizes speed and straightforward execution over flexibility, making it highly effective in local events.
win_conditions: Populating the board with a wide array of creatures
Winning through creature count and synergy
Executing sudden lethal damage with protected creatures

keep_criteria: Hands containing lords or early interaction
Hands with a mix of creatures and removal
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably shocklands/duals or fetches), a turn-1 play (Adeline, Resplendent Cathar, or Thalia's Lieutenant), and Dark Confidant if possible.
Prioritize hands that can play a threat on turn 1 or 2 while maintaining a 2-land curve.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early threats or lords
Hands heavy on late-game cards or sideboard-only spells
Hands with only lands or high-cost creatures
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only 2-drops+, or hands missing early threats and mana fixing.

examples.reason: Provides early aggression, a lord for buffs, and board interaction to protect the plan.

early_game: Play one-drops like Bloodsoaked Champion and Dauntless Bodyguard to establish early pressure
Cast lords like Thalia's Lieutenant and Rally the Ranks to buff the army
Use Kitesail Freebooter to gain information and force removal
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Dark Confidant or a 1-drop (Adeline/Cathar/Lieutenant).
Turn 2: Play a land, cast a 2-drop or attack with existing threats.
Sequence lands to ensure consistent early drops
fetch if you have a shockland in hand.
mid_game: Maintain board presence with recurring threats via Extraction Specialist
Play around sweepers using Mutavault and Selfless Spirit
Apply continuous pressure to force opponent answers
Treat Dark Confidant as a win condition and engine
protect it with lifegain or removal if possible, but don't over-invest if you're already ahead.
Manage hand size to avoid discarding key threats to discard spells.
Use lifegain from Cathar and Lieutenant to stabilize against burn and enable Confidant's draw engine.
Commit threats when you have removal or pump ready to protect them, or when the opponent has tapped out for answers.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers (Wrath of God, Day of Judgment) or heavy targeted removal.
late_game: Utilize Brave the Elements to protect creatures and enable sudden lethal
Deploy powerful lords like General Kudro of Drannith or Adeline, Resplendent Cathar to steamroll
Sideboard in graveyard hate and unconditional removal for control matchups
Adeline, Resplendent Cathar, and Thalia's Lieutenant with pump spells or +1/+1 counters.
Dark Confidant can also close games if left unchecked.
Use lifegain to stabilize while chipping away at life totals.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent has no sweepers, or you're at lethal range.
Commit when you can protect your board with lifegain, removal, or pump spells.
Don't hold back if the opponent is on a clock and you have the tools to close the game.

key_cards.name: Thalia, Guardian of Thraben
key_cards.notes: Staple for creature decks; significantly delays opponent spells and buffs Humans.

key_cards.name: Luminarch Aspirant
key_cards.notes: Forces an immediate answer to avoid steamrolling the game on its own.

key_cards.name: Extraction Specialist
key_cards.notes: Returns relevant non-attacking lords like Thalia's Lieutenant or Thalia to the battlefield.

key_cards.name: Dire Tactics
key_cards.notes: Preferred removal over Bloodchief's Thirst; interacts well with the go-wide proposal.

key_cards.name: Brave the Elements
key_cards.notes: Protects creatures from red sweepers and enables sudden lethal by giving protection from opponent's blocker colors.

key_cards.name: General Kudro of Drannith
key_cards.notes: Doubles as a recurring graveyard hate and a powerful lord, far more important than conditional removal.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize returning non-attacking lords like Thalia's Lieutenant or Thalia with Extraction Specialist
Manipulate the day and night cycle to optimize Brutal Cathar's protection
Use Brave the Elements to protect creatures from sweepers and enable sudden lethal
Play Mutavault to help play around sweepers while caring for creature types
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the board without considering sweepers or holding back too long against control.
Unique interaction: Thalia's Lieutenant's lifelink and +1/+1 counter synergy with Cathar's lifegain creates a resilient board that can outlast midrange and control.
Dark Confidant is a double-edged sword
manage its death trigger carefully and don't let it die to removal if you're already ahead.
Always track the opponent's graveyard against Greasefang and midrange decks.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Duress to remove sweepers, Selfless Spirit for protection, and graveyard hate like Graveyard Trespasser.
Use Extraction Specialist's lifelink to survive, and play around early damage with resilient creatures.
Sideboard in Containment Priest or Graveyard Trespasser to punish graveyard reliance.

Use cheap 1-drops that are hard to counter effectively, or sequence around counterspells by playing around them on turns they could have mana. Use pump spells or discard to disrupt their hand. Don't overcommit to a single threat if they have multiple counterspells.

Apply early pressure to beat their ramp out. Use removal for their mana dorks/acceleration. Don't overcommit if they have a massive finisher; stabilize with lifegain and trade efficiently. Force them to answer your threats instead of developing their board.

Use lifegain from Cathar and Lieutenant to stabilize. Hold removal for their key threats. Don't overextend into combat; trade efficiently and use discard to disrupt their game plan. Prioritize survival over aggression in the first few turns.

Win the resource war. Prioritize keeping Dark Confidant alive. Use discard to remove their threats. Trade efficiently and value out their Lieutenant/Cathar. Control the board with targeted removal and graveyard hate. Don't overcommit; value out their plays and close the game when you have an advantage.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, targeted removal (Path, Murder), and discard (Thoughtseize, Inquisition).
Note when opponents tap out for counterspells or answers.
Track graveyard usage against Greasefang and midrange decks.
Pay attention to when the opponent holds up mana for multiple turns, indicating a potential board wipe or finisher.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Betting on populating the board with creatures and winning the game through the amount and synergy between its cards... this is a beatdown archetype

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: winning the game through the amount and synergy between its cards... making it easy to remove Planeswalkers from by attacking them

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Your priority targets are those that don't need to attack to be useful... how easily this archetype can manipulate the day and night cycle

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: protect creatures from sweepers like Supreme Verdict... red sweepers like Anger of the Gods... deals with Planeswalkers
